title: Bridge Essay: Reconstructing and deconstructing the self: cognitive mechanisms in meditation p

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Form/Meditation** (Phase 7) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Form/Meditation to mechanistic neuroscience through Reconstructing, and, deconstructing, the, self:.

**Reconstructing and deconstructing the self: cognitive mechanisms in meditation practice.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 26231761. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2015.07.001. Scientific research highlights the central role of specific psychological processes, in particular those related to the self, in various forms of human suffering and flourishing. This view is shared by Buddhism and other contemplative and humanistic traditions, which have developed meditation practices to regulate these processes.
